title: eriogonum umbellatum var. cognatum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum var. cognatum, also known as Sulfur Buckwheat or Sulfur Flower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Eriogonum genus. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Eriogonum umbellatum var. cognatum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically in salves and creams for its medicinal properties.
